title: Client-side dataset backup/restore + in-repo personal-use documentation

requirements.summary: Add browser-storage backup plus download/restore actions to recover the last imported spare-parts dataset when backend data is missing/expired.
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful import, the app stores a local backup of the imported spare parts in browser storage.
The Upload area provides a clearly labeled action to download the locally stored dataset as a JSON file.

If the backend returns zero parts but a local backup exists, the Upload area provides a clearly labeled action to restore/re-import the local backup to the backend (requires the existing unlock/password flow).
Restoring from backup updates the table and import status to match the restored dataset.

file_operations.description: Create small utilities for (a) persisting the last imported SparePart[] into browser storage (localStorage), (b) reading/validating it back, and (c) exporting/importing a JSON backup payload. Keep payload versioned and include minimal metadata (e.g., savedAt, recordCount) to support future compatibility.

Persist a local backup after a successful backend import by hooking into the existing React Query import mutation lifecycle (use onSuccess with the mutation variables) and saving the imported SparePart[] via the new backup utility. Also invalidate/refetch remains as-is so the UI updates from backend state after restore/import.

Extend the Upload area UI to (1) show a clearly labeled “Download local backup (JSON)” action when a local backup exists, and (2) show a clearly labeled “Restore from local backup” action when backend currently has zero parts but a local backup exists. The restore action must use the existing maintenance unlock/password flow (disable or gate the restore button until unlocked) and then re-upload the locally saved SparePart[] via the existing import mutation. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Alert, Card, Separator) for consistent styling and affordances. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure the app’s empty/loaded states correctly reflect a restore: after a restore/import completes, the table renders with restored data and the import status updates (primarily via the existing query invalidation/refetch behavior). If needed, adjust the empty-state gating logic to avoid confusing transitions when a restore is initiated.

requirements.summary: Add English, personal-use run/deploy and data persistence/backup documentation in-repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository contains clear English documentation explaining how to run the frontend locally and deploy the backend canister using dfx.
Documentation explains how spare-parts data is stored, how long it is retained, what happens on upgrade, and how to use the new backup/restore flow.
Documentation includes a brief section explaining Draft vs Live environments and the impact on data availability (at a practical, user-action level).

file_operations.description: Add English personal-use documentation covering: local frontend run steps; deploying to the Internet Computer with dfx (high-level commands and expected outcomes); how spare-parts data is retained (7-day logic) and the practical meaning of backend upgrades; how the client-side backup works (localStorage + JSON download) and how to restore (requires unlock/password); and a Draft vs Live section describing data availability implications and recommended user actions (e.g., publish Live to avoid draft expiry).
